Followed official AIDA links to classroom, student, faculty/staff, TerrierGPT, and FAQ pages for direct official-source evidence.

artifacts.summary: Firecrawl discovery and fetches found central official Boston University AIDA guidance for classroom use, student use, faculty and staff use, TerrierGPT, and FAQ content. Faculty-submitted sample syllabi and the BU Today article about the AI Task Force report were recorded as official-but-unsuitable sources and were not used for university-wide claim evidence.

stopConditions: Stop after central official Boston University AIDA sources provide direct narrow support for the staged claims.

Do not bypass login, CAPTCHA, WAF, robots, paywall, or other access restrictions.

artifacts.claimText: Boston University AIDA classroom guidance recommends that faculty state their GenAI policy explicitly in the course syllabus, disclose how instructors will use GenAI for course tasks, explain the policy in the first week, and distinguish acceptable from unacceptable uses.

artifacts.claimText: Boston University student guidance says instructors have broad discretion to set GenAI rules within each course, and students are responsible for complying with those instructions and should consult the course GenAI policy or ask the instructor before assuming GenAI use is allowed.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI Guidelines for Classroom Use
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: State your policy on GenAI use explicitly in the course syllabus. This includes disclosing how the instructors (faculty and student teachers) will use GenAI for lecture preparation, presentations, grading, and other course related tasks. Take time in the first week of class to explain your policy and its rationale. Make clear distinctions between acceptable and unacceptable uses.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Instructors have broad discretion to set rules on how GenAI may or may not be used within each individual course. It is your responsibility to comply with these instructions. Always consult the GenAI policy for the course or ask the instructor before assuming use of GenAI is allowed.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI Guidelines for BU Faculty & Staff
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Avoid inputting or sharing private or sensitive information through commercial GenAI tools, as this could violate privacy laws or university policy. For institutional use, TerrierGPT is recommended because it protects university data and complies with internal privacy policies. However, TerrierGPT is not approved for restricted use data.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: TerrierGPT complies with BU's internal privacy and data protection policies-and none of the data entered is used to train external models. Data uploaded to the platform is only accessible by IS&T personnel and has the same strong privacy protections applicable to all BU enterprise data... the platform is approved for data classified as confidential, but not restricted use.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: AI is rapidly evolving, and BU faculty members may freely decide how to set the AI policies for each of their courses, within broad limits established by the BU Academic Conduct Code. Variation across courses is the norm, not the exception. Students are encouraged to review the course policies and consult their instructors for guidance.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI Guidelines for Classroom Use
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Be very cautious with accusations of GenAI misuse-all detection tools are highly fallible, both with respect to false positives and false negatives, despite the marketing claims of companies that sell these products. Apply enforcement policies uniformly to minimize bias.

artifacts.notes: Claim is framed as an AIDA recommendation and is limited to the classroom syllabus-policy evidence.
Claim preserves the course-specific scope and student responsibility language from the official student guidance.
Claim directly tracks the official student guidance on disclosure, attribution, plagiarism, and academic misconduct.

Claim is limited to the privacy/security passage and preserves the TerrierGPT restricted-use-data limit.

Claim is limited to the FAQ answer about course-level AI-policy discretion and Academic Conduct Code boundaries.

artifacts.title: Boston University AI Policy Staged Review
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for Boston University uses central official AIDA pages on classroom use, student guidelines, faculty and staff guidelines, TerrierGPT, and FAQ content. The claim set covers recommended course syllabus policy disclosure, student responsibility for course-specific GenAI rules, attribution and academic misconduct, human oversight and transparency, privacy/security limits for commercial GenAI and TerrierGPT, TerrierGPT data-security boundaries, faculty course-policy latitude, and caution around AI detector-based misconduct accusations.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are staged candidates only and do not set publishAsCanonical or isCanonical.
No human review is asserted; reviewState is agent_reviewed only for direct official-source evidence.

The FAQ page initially returned a temporary Boston University unavailable page through Firecrawl, then returned the real FAQ content on a retry; only the successful official FAQ content is used for evidence.
The official sample syllabi page was rejected for university-wide claim support because it contains faculty-submitted course examples.
The official BU Today AI Task Force article was rejected for claim support because it summarizes recommendations/news rather than serving as current guidance.
No separate central, current research-specific binding GenAI policy was identified in this pass; research references are limited to the broader faculty and staff guidance.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
